**v5** "Go back and tell Hezekiah the leader of My people that this is what the LORD, the God of your father David, says: 'I have heard your prayer; I have seen your tears. I will surely heal you. On the third day from now you will go up to the house of the LORD.
**v6** I will add fifteen years to your life. And I will deliver you and this city from the hand of the king of Assyria. I will defend this city for My sake and for the sake of My servant David.'"

**v7** Then Isaiah said, "Prepare a poultice of figs." So they brought it and applied it to the boil, and Hezekiah recovered.

**v8** Now Hezekiah had asked Isaiah, "What will be the sign that the LORD will heal me and that I will go up to the house of the LORD on the third day?"

**v9** And Isaiah had replied, "This will be a sign to you from the LORD that He will do what He has promised: Would you like the shadow to go forward ten steps, or back ten steps?"

**v10** "It is easy for the shadow to lengthen ten steps," answered Hezekiah, "but not for it to go back ten steps."

**v11** So Isaiah the prophet called out to the LORD, and He brought the shadow back the ten steps it had descended on the stairway of Ahaz.
#### Hezekiah Shows His Treasures

**v12** At that time Merodach-baladan [^2] son of Baladan king of Babylon sent letters and a gift to Hezekiah, for he had heard about Hezekiah's illness.
**v13** And Hezekiah received the envoys and showed them all that was in his treasure house—the silver, the gold, the spices, and the precious oil, as well as his armory—all that was found in his storehouses. There was nothing in his palace or in all his dominion that Hezekiah did not show them.

**v14** Then the prophet Isaiah went to King Hezekiah and asked, "Where did those men come from, and what did they say to you?""They came from a distant land," Hezekiah replied, "from Babylon."

**v15** "What have they seen in your palace?" Isaiah asked." They have seen everything in my palace," answered Hezekiah. "There is nothing among my treasures that I did not show them."

**v16** Then Isaiah said to Hezekiah, "Hear the word of the LORD:
**v17** The time will surely come when everything in your palace and all that your fathers have stored up until this day will be carried off to Babylon. Nothing will be left, says the LORD.
**v18** And some of your descendants, your own flesh and blood, will be taken away to be eunuchs in the palace of the king of Babylon."

**v19** But Hezekiah said to Isaiah, "The word of the LORD that you have spoken is good." For he thought, "Will there not at least be peace and security in my lifetime?"
#### Manasseh Succeeds Hezekiah

**v20** As for the rest of the acts of Hezekiah, along with all his might and how he constructed the pool and the tunnel [^3] to bring water into the city, are they not written in the Book of the Chronicles of the Kings of Judah?

**v21** And Hezekiah rested with his fathers, and his son Manasseh reigned in his place.
[^1]: LXX and an alternate MT reading; the other alternate reads the middle of the city ( 20:4)
[^2]: Some Hebrew manuscripts, LXX, and Syriac (see also Isaiah 39:1); MT Berodach-baladan ( 20:12)
[^3]: Or watercourse or conduit ( 20:20)

### Chapter 21

#### Manasseh Reigns in Judah
**v1** Manasseh was twelve years old when he became king, and he reigned in Jerusalem fifty-five years. His mother's name was Hephzibah.
**v2** And he did evil in the sight of the LORD by following the abominations of the nations that the LORD had driven out before the Israelites.
**v3** For he rebuilt the high places that his father Hezekiah had destroyed, and he raised up altars for Baal. He made an Asherah pole, as King Ahab of Israel had done, and he worshiped and served all the host of heaven.

**v4** Manasseh also built altars in the house of the LORD, of which the LORD had said, "In Jerusalem I will put My Name."
**v5** In both courtyards of the house of the LORD, he built altars to all the host of heaven.
**v6** He sacrificed his own son in the fire, [^1] practiced sorcery and divination, and consulted mediums and spiritists. He did great evil in the sight of the LORD, provoking Him to anger.

**v7** Manasseh even took the carved Asherah pole he had made and set it up in the temple, of which the LORD had said to David and his son Solomon, "In this temple and in Jerusalem, which I have chosen out of all the tribes of Israel, I will establish My Name forever.
**v8** I will never again cause the feet of the Israelites to wander from the land that I gave to their fathers, if only they are careful to do all I have commanded them—the whole Law that My servant Moses commanded them."

**v9** But the people did not listen and Manasseh led them astray, so that they did greater evil than the nations that the LORD had destroyed before the Israelites.
#### Manasseh's Idolatries Rebuked

**v10** And the LORD spoke through His servants the prophets, saying,
**v11** "Since Manasseh king of Judah has committed all these abominations, acting more wickedly than the Amorites who preceded him, and with his idols has caused Judah to sin,
**v12** this is what the LORD, the God of Israel, says: 'Behold, I am bringing such calamity upon Jerusalem and Judah that the news will reverberate in the ears of all who hear it.

**v13** I will stretch out over Jerusalem the measuring line used against Samaria and the plumb line used against the house of Ahab, and I will wipe out Jerusalem as one wipes out a bowl—wiping it and turning it upside down.
**v14** So I will forsake the remnant of My inheritance and deliver them into the hands of their enemies. And they will become plunder and spoil to all their enemies,
**v15** because they have done evil in My sight and have provoked Me to anger from the day their fathers came out of Egypt until this day.'"

**v16** Moreover, Manasseh shed so much innocent blood that he filled Jerusalem from end to end, in addition to the sin that he had caused Judah to commit, doing evil in the sight of the LORD.

**v17** As for the rest of the acts of Manasseh, along with all his accomplishments and the sin that he committed, are they not written in the Book of the Chronicles of the Kings of Judah?

**v18** And Manasseh rested with his fathers and was buried in his palace garden, the garden of Uzza. And his son Amon reigned in his place.
#### Amon Reigns in Judah

**v19** Amon was twenty-two years old when he became king, and he reigned in Jerusalem two years. His mother's name was Meshullemeth daughter of Haruz; she was from Jotbah.
**v20** And he did evil in the sight of the LORD, as his father Manasseh had done.
**v21** He walked in all the ways of his father, and he served and worshiped the idols his father had served.
**v22** He abandoned the LORD, the God of his fathers, and did not walk in the way of the LORD.

**v23** Then the servants of Amon conspired against him and killed the king in his palace.
**v24** But the people of the land killed all those who had conspired against King Amon, and they made his son Josiah king in his place.

**v25** As for the rest of the acts of Amon, along with his accomplishments, are they not written in the Book of the Chronicles of the Kings of Judah?
**v26** And he was buried in his tomb in the garden of Uzza, and his son Josiah reigned in his place.
[^1]: Literally made his son pass through the fire ( 21:6)

### Chapter 22

#### Josiah Reigns in Judah
**v1** Josiah was eight years old when he became king, and he reigned in Jerusalem thirty-one years. His mother's name was Jedidah daughter of Adaiah; she was from Bozkath.
**v2** And he did what was right in the eyes of the LORD and walked in all the ways of his father David; he did not turn aside to the right or to the left.
#### Funding the Temple Repairs

**v3** Now in the eighteenth year of his reign, King Josiah sent the scribe, Shaphan son of Azaliah, the son of Meshullam, to the house of the LORD, saying,
**v4** "Go up to Hilkiah the high priest and have him count the money that has been brought into the house of the LORD, which the doorkeepers have collected from the people.
**v5** And let them deliver it into the hands of the supervisors of those doing the work on the house of the LORD, who in turn are to give it to the workmen repairing the damages to the house of the LORD—
**v6** to the carpenters, builders, and masons—to buy timber and dressed stone to repair the temple.
**v7** But they need not account for the money put into their hands, since they work with integrity."
#### Hilkiah Finds the Book of the Law

**v8** Then Hilkiah the high priest said to Shaphan the scribe, "I have found the Book of the Law in the house of the LORD!" And he gave it to Shaphan, who read it.

**v9** And Shaphan the scribe went to the king and reported, "Your servants have paid out the money that was found in the temple and have put it into the hands of the workers and supervisors of the house of the LORD."

**v10** Moreover, Shaphan the scribe told the king, "Hilkiah the priest has given me a book." And Shaphan read it in the presence of the king.

**v11** When the king heard the words of the Book of the Law, he tore his clothes
**v12** and commanded Hilkiah the priest, Ahikam son of Shaphan, Achbor [^1] son of Micaiah, [^2] Shaphan the scribe, and Asaiah the servant of the king:
**v13** "Go and inquire of the LORD for me, for the people, and for all Judah concerning the words in this book that has been found. For great is the wrath of the LORD that burns against us because our fathers have not obeyed the words of this book by doing all that is written about us."
#### Huldah's Prophecy

**v14** So Hilkiah the priest, Ahikam, Achbor, Shaphan, and Asaiah went and spoke to Huldah the prophetess, the wife of Shallum son of Tikvah, [^3] the son of Harhas, [^4] the keeper of the wardrobe. She lived in Jerusalem, in the Second District. [^5]

**v15** And Huldah said to them, "This is what the LORD, the God of Israel, says: 'Tell the man who sent you
**v16** that this is what the LORD says: I am about to bring calamity on this place and on its people, according to all the words of the book that the king of Judah has read,
**v17** because they have forsaken Me and burned incense to other gods, that they might provoke Me to anger with all the works of their hands. My wrath will be kindled against this place and will not be quenched.'

**v18** But as for the king of Judah, who sent you to inquire of the LORD, tell him that this is what the LORD, the God of Israel, says: 'As for the words that you heard,
**v19** because your heart was tender and you humbled yourself before the LORD when you heard what I spoke against this place and against its people, that they would become a desolation and a curse, and because you have torn your clothes and wept before Me, I have heard you,' declares the LORD.

**v20** 'Therefore I will indeed gather you to your fathers, and you will be gathered to your grave in peace. Your eyes will not see all the calamity that I will bring on this place.'"So they brought her answer back to the king.
[^1]: Achbor is another name for Abdon; see 2 Chronicles 34:20. ( 22:12)
[^2]: Micaiah is a variant of Micah; see 2 Chronicles 34:20. ( 22:12)
[^3]: Tikvah is a variant of Tokhath; see 2 Chronicles 34:22. ( 22:14)
[^4]: Harhas is a variant of Hasrah; see 2 Chronicles 34:22. ( 22:14)
[^5]: Or the Second Quarter, a newer section of Jerusalem; Hebrew the Mishneh ( 22:14)

### Chapter 23

#### Josiah Renews the Covenant
**v1** Then the king summoned all the elders of Judah and Jerusalem.
**v2** And he went up to the house of the LORD with all the people of Judah and Jerusalem, as well as the priests and the prophets—all the people small and great—and in their hearing he read all the words of the Book of the Covenant that had been found in the house of the LORD.

**v3** So the king stood by the pillar and made a covenant before the LORD to follow the LORD and to keep His commandments, decrees, and statutes with all his heart and all his soul, and to carry out the words of the covenant that were written in this book. And all the people entered into the covenant.
#### Josiah Destroys Idolatry

**v4** Then the king commanded Hilkiah the high priest, the priests second in rank, and the doorkeepers to remove from the temple of the LORD all the articles made for Baal, Asherah, and all the host of heaven. And he burned them outside Jerusalem in the fields of Kidron and carried their ashes to Bethel.

**v5** Josiah also did away with the idolatrous priests ordained by the kings of Judah to burn incense on the high places of the cities of Judah and in the places all around Jerusalem—those who had burned incense to Baal, to the sun and moon, to the constellations, and to all the host of heaven.

**v6** He brought the Asherah pole from the house of the LORD to the Kidron Valley outside Jerusalem, and there he burned it, ground it to powder, and threw its dust on the graves of the common people.
**v7** He also tore down the quarters of the male shrine prostitutes that were in the house of the LORD, where the women had woven tapestries for Asherah.

**v8** Then Josiah brought all the priests from the cities of Judah and desecrated the high places, from Geba to Beersheba, where the priests had burned incense. He tore down the high places of the gates at the entrance of the gate of Joshua the governor of the city, which was to the left of the city gate.
**v9** Although the priests of the high places did not come up to the altar of the LORD in Jerusalem, they ate unleavened bread with their fellow priests.

**v10** He also desecrated Topheth in the Valley of Ben-hinnom [^1] so that no one could sacrifice his son or daughter in the fire [^2] to Molech.
**v11** And he removed from the entrance to the house of the LORD the horses that the kings of Judah had dedicated to the sun. They were in the court [^3] near the chamber of an official named Nathan-melech. And Josiah burned up the chariots of the sun.

**v12** He pulled down the altars that the kings of Judah had set up on the roof near the upper chamber of Ahaz, and the altars that Manasseh had set up in the two courtyards of the house of the LORD. The king pulverized them there [^4] and threw their dust into the Kidron Valley.

**v13** The king also desecrated the high places east of Jerusalem, to the south of the Mount of Corruption, which King Solomon of Israel had built for Ashtoreth the abomination of the Sidonians, for Chemosh the abomination of the Moabites, and for Milcom [^5] the abomination of the Ammonites.
**v14** He smashed the sacred pillars to pieces, cut down the Asherah poles, and covered the sites with human bones.

**v15** He even pulled down the altar at Bethel, the high place set up by Jeroboam son of Nebat, who had caused Israel to sin. Then he burned [^6] the high place, ground it to powder, and burned the Asherah pole.
**v16** And as Josiah turned, he saw the tombs there on the hillside, and he sent someone to take the bones out of the tombs, and he burned them on the altar to defile it, according to the word of the LORD proclaimed by the man of God who had foretold these things. [^7]

**v17** Then the king asked, "What is this monument I see?" And the men of the city replied, "It is the tomb of the man of God who came from Judah and proclaimed these things that you have done to the altar of Bethel."

**v18** "Let him rest," said Josiah. "Do not let anyone disturb his bones." So they left his bones undisturbed, along with those of the prophet who had come from Samaria.

**v19** Just as Josiah had done at Bethel, so also in the cities of Samaria he removed all the shrines of the high places set up by the kings of Israel who had provoked the LORD to anger.
**v20** On the altars he slaughtered all the priests of the high places, and he burned human bones on them. Then he returned to Jerusalem.
#### Josiah Restores the Passover

**v21** The king commanded all the people, "Keep the Passover of the LORD your God, as it is written in this Book of the Covenant."

**v22** No such Passover had been observed from the days of the judges who had governed Israel through all the days of the kings of Israel and Judah.
**v23** But in the eighteenth year of Josiah's reign, this Passover was observed to the LORD in Jerusalem.

**v24** Furthermore, Josiah removed the mediums and spiritists, the household gods and idols, and all the abominations that were seen in the land of Judah and in Jerusalem. He did this to carry out the words of the law written in the book that Hilkiah the priest had found in the house of the LORD.

**v25** Neither before nor after Josiah was there any king like him, who turned to the LORD with all his heart and with all his soul and with all his strength, according to all the Law of Moses.

**v26** Nevertheless, the LORD did not turn away from the fury of His burning anger, which was kindled against Judah because of all that Manasseh had done to provoke Him to anger.
**v27** For the LORD had said, "I will remove Judah from My sight, just as I removed Israel. I will reject this city Jerusalem, which I chose, and the temple of which I said, 'My Name shall be there.'"
#### The Death of Josiah

**v28** As for the rest of the acts of Josiah and all his accomplishments, are they not written in the Book of the Chronicles of the Kings of Judah?

**v29** During Josiah's reign, Pharaoh Neco king of Egypt marched up to help the king of Assyria at the Euphrates River. King Josiah went out to confront him, but Neco faced him and killed him at Megiddo.

**v30** From Megiddo his servants carried his body in a chariot, brought him to Jerusalem, and buried him in his own tomb. Then the people of the land took Jehoahaz son of Josiah, anointed him, and made him king in place of his father.
#### Jehoahaz Succeeds Josiah

**v31** Jehoahaz was twenty-three years old when he became king, and he reigned in Jerusalem three months. His mother's name was Hamutal daughter of Jeremiah; she was from Libnah.
**v32** And he did evil in the sight of the LORD, just as his fathers had done.

**v33** And Pharaoh Neco imprisoned Jehoahaz at Riblah in the land of Hamath so that he could not reign in Jerusalem, and he imposed on Judah a levy of a hundred talents of silver [^8] and a talent of gold. [^9]
**v34** Then Pharaoh Neco made Eliakim son of Josiah king in place of his father Josiah, and he changed Eliakim's name to Jehoiakim. But Neco took Jehoahaz and carried him off to Egypt, where he died.

**v35** So Jehoiakim paid the silver and gold to Pharaoh Neco, but to meet Pharaoh's demand he taxed the land and exacted the silver and the gold from the people, each according to his wealth.
#### Jehoiakim Reigns in Judah

**v36** Jehoiakim was twenty-five years old when he became king, and he reigned in Jerusalem eleven years. His mother's name was Zebidah daughter of Pedaiah; she was from Rumah.
**v37** And he did evil in the sight of the LORD, just as his fathers had done.
[^1]: Or the Valley of the Son of Hinnom ( 23:10)
[^2]: Literally could pass his son or daughter through the fire ( 23:10)
[^3]: Hebrew parbarim ( 23:11)
[^4]: Or quickly removed them from there ( 23:12)
[^5]: Milcom is a variant of Molech; see Leviticus 18:21 and 1 Kings 11:7. ( 23:13)
[^6]: Hebrew; LXX broke into pieces ( 23:15)
[^7]: See 1 Kings 13:2; Hebrew; LXX includes when Jeroboam stood by the altar at the feast. And he turned and lifted his eyes to the tomb of the man of God. ( 23:16)
[^8]: 100 talents is approximately 3.77 tons or 3.42 metric tons of silver. ( 23:33)
[^9]: A talent is approximately 75.4 pounds or 34.2 kilograms of gold. ( 23:33)

### Chapter 24

#### Babylon Controls Jehoiakim
**v1** During Jehoiakim's reign, Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon invaded. So Jehoiakim became his vassal for three years, until he turned and rebelled against Nebuchadnezzar.

**v2** And the LORD sent Chaldean, [^1] Aramean, Moabite, and Ammonite raiders against Jehoiakim in order to destroy Judah, according to the word that the LORD had spoken through His servants the prophets.
**v3** Surely this happened to Judah at the LORD's command, to remove them from His presence because of the sins of Manasseh and all that he had done,
**v4** and also for the innocent blood he had shed. For he had filled Jerusalem with innocent blood, and the LORD was unwilling to forgive.

**v5** As for the rest of the acts of Jehoiakim, along with all his accomplishments, are they not written in the Book of the Chronicles of the Kings of Judah?
#### Jehoiachin Reigns in Judah

**v6** And Jehoiakim rested with his fathers, and his son Jehoiachin reigned in his place.

**v7** Now the king of Egypt did not march out of his land again, because the king of Babylon had taken all his territory, from the Brook of Egypt to the Euphrates River.

**v8** Jehoiachin was eighteen years old when he became king, and he reigned in Jerusalem three months. His mother's name was Nehushta daughter of Elnathan; she was from Jerusalem.
**v9** And he did evil in the sight of the LORD, just as his father had done.
#### The Captivity of Jerusalem

**v10** At that time the servants of Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon marched up to Jerusalem, and the city came under siege.
**v11** And Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon came to the city while his servants were besieging it.
**v12** Jehoiachin king of Judah, his mother, his servants, his commanders, and his officials all surrendered to the king of Babylon. So in the eighth year of his reign, the king of Babylon took him captive.
**v13** As the LORD had declared, Nebuchadnezzar also carried off all the treasures from the house of the LORD and the royal palace, and he cut into pieces all the gold articles that Solomon king of Israel had made in the temple of the LORD.
**v14** He carried into exile all Jerusalem—all the commanders and mighty men of valor, all the craftsmen and metalsmiths—ten thousand captives in all. Only the poorest people of the land remained.

**v15** Nebuchadnezzar carried away Jehoiachin to Babylon, as well as the king's mother, his wives, his officials, and the leading men of the land. He took them into exile from Jerusalem to Babylon.
**v16** The king of Babylon also brought into exile to Babylon all seven thousand men of valor and a thousand craftsmen and metalsmiths—all strong and fit for battle.

**v17** Then the king of Babylon made Mattaniah, Jehoiachin's uncle, king in his place and changed his name to Zedekiah.
#### Zedekiah Reigns in Judah

**v18** Zedekiah was twenty-one years old when he became king, and he reigned in Jerusalem eleven years. His mother's name was Hamutal daughter of Jeremiah; she was from Libnah.

**v19** And Zedekiah did evil in the sight of the LORD, just as Jehoiakim had done.
**v20** For because of the anger of the LORD, all this happened in Jerusalem and Judah, until He finally banished them from His presence. And Zedekiah also rebelled against the king of Babylon.
[^1]: Or Babylonian ( 24:2)

### Chapter 25

#### Nebuchadnezzar Besieges Jerusalem
**v1** So in the ninth year of Zedekiah's reign, on the tenth day of the tenth month, Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon marched against Jerusalem with his entire army. They encamped outside the city and built [^1] a siege wall all around it.
**v2** And the city was kept under siege until King Zedekiah's eleventh year.

**v3** By the ninth day of the fourth month, [^2] the famine in the city was so severe that the people of the land had no food.
**v4** Then the city was breached; and though the Chaldeans [^3] had surrounded the city, all the men of war fled by night by way of the gate between the two walls near the king's garden. They headed toward the Arabah, [^4]
**v5** but the army of the Chaldeans pursued the king and overtook him in the plains of Jericho, and his whole army deserted him.
**v6** The Chaldeans seized the king and brought him up to the king of Babylon at Riblah, where they pronounced judgment on him.
**v7** And they slaughtered the sons of Zedekiah before his eyes. Then they put out his eyes, bound him with bronze shackles, and took him to Babylon.
#### The Temple Destroyed

**v8** On the seventh day of the fifth month, in the nineteenth year of Nebuchadnezzar's reign over Babylon, Nebuzaradan captain of the guard, a servant of the king of Babylon, entered Jerusalem.
**v9** He burned down the house of the LORD, the royal palace, and all the houses of Jerusalem—every significant building.
**v10** And the whole army of the Chaldeans under the captain of the guard broke down the walls around Jerusalem.

**v11** Then Nebuzaradan captain of the guard carried into exile the people who remained in the city, along with the deserters who had defected to the king of Babylon and the rest of the population.
**v12** But the captain of the guard left behind some of the poorest of the land to tend the vineyards and fields.

**v13** Moreover, the Chaldeans broke up the bronze pillars and stands and the bronze Sea in the house of the LORD, and they carried the bronze to Babylon.
**v14** They also took away the pots, shovels, wick trimmers, dishes, and all the articles of bronze used in the temple service.
**v15** The captain of the guard also took away the censers and sprinkling bowls—anything made of pure gold or fine silver.

**v16** As for the two pillars, the Sea, and the movable stands that Solomon had made for the house of the LORD, the weight of the bronze from all these articles was beyond measure.
**v17** Each pillar was eighteen cubits tall. [^5] The bronze capital atop one pillar was three cubits high, [^6] with a network of bronze pomegranates all around. The second pillar, with its network, was similar.
#### Captives Carried to Babylon

**v18** The captain of the guard also took away Seraiah the chief priest, Zephaniah the priest of second rank, and the three doorkeepers.
**v19** Of those still in the city, he took a court official who had been appointed over the men of war, as well as five royal advisors. He also took the scribe of the captain of the army, who had enlisted the people of the land, and sixty men who were found in the city.

**v20** Nebuzaradan captain of the guard took them and brought them to the king of Babylon at Riblah.
**v21** There at Riblah in the land of Hamath, the king of Babylon struck them down and put them to death. So Judah was taken into exile, away from its own land.
#### Gedaliah Governs in Judah

**v22** Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon appointed Gedaliah son of Ahikam, the son of Shaphan, over the people he had left behind in the land of Judah.

**v23** When all the commanders of the armies and their men heard that the king of Babylon had appointed Gedaliah as governor, they came to Gedaliah at Mizpah—Ishmael son of Nethaniah, Johanan son of Kareah, Seraiah son of Tanhumeth the Netophathite, and Jaazaniah [^7] son of the Maacathite, as well as their men.
**v24** And Gedaliah took an oath before them and their men, assuring them, "Do not be afraid of the servants of the Chaldeans. Live in the land and serve the king of Babylon, and it will be well with you."
#### The Murder of Gedaliah

**v25** In the seventh month, however, Ishmael son of Nethaniah, the son of Elishama, who was a member of the royal family, came with ten men and struck down and killed Gedaliah, along with the Judeans and Chaldeans who were with him at Mizpah.
**v26** Then all the people small and great, together with the commanders of the army, arose and fled to Egypt for fear of the Chaldeans.
#### Jehoiachin Released from Prison

**v27** On the twenty-seventh day of the twelfth month of the thirty-seventh year of the exile of Judah's King Jehoiachin, in the year Evil-merodach became king of Babylon, he released [^8] King Jehoiachin of Judah from prison.
**v28** And he spoke kindly to Jehoiachin and set his throne above the thrones of the other kings who were with him in Babylon.

**v29** So Jehoiachin changed out of his prison clothes, and he dined regularly at the king's table for the rest of his life.
**v30** And the king provided Jehoiachin a daily portion for the rest of his life.
[^1]: Literally He encamped outside it and they built ( 25:1)
[^2]: Probable reading (see Jeremiah 52:6); MT does not include fourth. ( 25:3)
[^3]: That is, the Babylonians; also in verses 5, 6, 10, 13, 24, 25, and 26 ( 25:4)
[^4]: Or the Jordan Valley ( 25:4)
[^5]: 18 cubits is approximately 27 feet or 8.2 meters. ( 25:17)
[^6]: 3 cubits is approximately 4.5 feet or 1.4 meters. ( 25:17)
[^7]: Jaazaniah is a variant of Jezaniah; see Jeremiah 40:8. ( 25:23)
[^8]: Literally lifted up the head of ( 25:27)

## 1 Chronicles

### Chapter 1

#### From Adam to Abraham
**v1** Adam, Seth, Enosh,

**v2** Kenan, Mahalalel, Jared,

**v3** Enoch, Methuselah, Lamech, Noah.

**v4** The sons of Noah: [^1] Shem, Ham, and Japheth.

**v5** The sons of Japheth:Gomer, Magog, Madai, Javan, Tubal, Meshech, and Tiras.

**v6** The sons of Gomer: Ashkenaz, Riphath, [^2] and Togarmah.

**v7** And the sons of Javan: Elishah, Tarshish, the Kittites, and the Rodanites.

**v8** The sons of Ham:Cush, Mizraim, Put, and Canaan.

**v9** The sons of Cush: Seba, Havilah, Sabta, [^3] Raamah, and Sabteca. The sons of Raamah: Sheba and Dedan.

**v10** Cush was the father of Nimrod, who began to be a mighty one [^4] on the earth.

**v11** Mizraim was the father of the Ludites, the Anamites, the Lehabites, the Naphtuhites,
**v12** the Pathrusites, the Casluhites (from whom the Philistines came), and the Caphtorites. [^5]

**v13** And Canaan was the father of Sidon his firstborn, [^6] and of the Hittites, [^7]
**v14** the Jebusites, the Amorites, the Girgashites,
**v15** the Hivites, the Arkites, the Sinites,
**v16** the Arvadites, the Zemarites, and the Hamathites.

**v17** The sons of Shem:Elam, Asshur, Arphaxad, Lud, and Aram. The sons of Aram: [^8] Uz, Hul, Gether, and Meshech. [^9]

**v18** Arphaxad was the father of Shelah, and Shelah was the father of Eber.

**v19** Two sons were born to Eber: One was named Peleg, [^10] because in his days the earth was divided, and his brother was named Joktan.

**v20** And Joktan was the father of Almodad, Sheleph, Hazarmaveth, Jerah,
**v21** Hadoram, Uzal, Diklah,
**v22** Obal, [^11] Abimael, Sheba,
**v23** Ophir, Havilah, and Jobab. All these were sons of Joktan.

**v24** So from Shem came Arphaxad, Shelah, [^12]
**v25** Eber, Peleg, Reu,
**v26** Serug, Nahor, Terah,
**v27** and Abram (that is, Abraham).
#### The Descendants of Abraham

**v28** The sons of Abraham were Isaac and Ishmael.
**v29** These are their genealogies:Nebaioth the firstborn of Ishmael, then Kedar, Adbeel, Mibsam,
**v30** Mishma, Dumah, Massa, Hadad, Tema,
**v31** Jetur, Naphish, and Kedemah. These were the sons of Ishmael.

**v32** The sons born to Keturah, Abraham's concubine:Zimran, Jokshan, Medan, Midian, Ishbak, and Shuah. The sons of Jokshan:Sheba and Dedan.

**v33** The sons of Midian:Ephah, Epher, Hanoch, Abida, and Eldaah. All of these were Keturah's sons.

**v34** Abraham was the father of Isaac. The sons of Isaac:Esau and Israel.
#### The Descendants of Esau

**v35** The sons of Esau:Eliphaz, Reuel, Jeush, Jalam, and Korah.

**v36** The sons of Eliphaz: Teman, Omar, Zepho, [^13] Gatam, and Kenaz; and by Timna, Amalek.

**v37** The sons of Reuel: Nahath, Zerah, Shammah, and Mizzah.
#### The Descendants of Seir

**v38** The sons of Seir:Lotan, Shobal, Zibeon, Anah, Dishon, Ezer, and Dishan.

**v39** The sons of Lotan: Hori and Homam. [^14] Timna was Lotan's sister.

**v40** The sons of Shobal: Alvan, [^15] Manahath, Ebal, Shepho, [^16] and Onam. The sons of Zibeon: Aiah and Anah.

**v41** The son [^17] of Anah: Dishon. The sons of Dishon: Hemdan, [^18] Eshban, Ithran, and Cheran.

**v42** The sons of Ezer: Bilhan, Zaavan, and Akan. [^19] The sons of Dishan: Uz and Aran.
#### The Kings of Edom

**v43** These are the kings who reigned in the land of Edom before any king reigned over the Israelites: [^20] Bela son of Beor. His city was named Dinhabah.

**v44** When Bela died, Jobab son of Zerah from Bozrah reigned in his place.

**v45** When Jobab died, Husham from the land of the Temanites reigned in his place.

**v46** When Husham died, Hadad son of Bedad, who defeated Midian in the country of Moab, reigned in his place. And the name of his city was Avith.

**v47** When Hadad died, Samlah from Masrekah reigned in his place.

**v48** When Samlah died, Shaul from Rehoboth on the Euphrates [^21] reigned in his place.

**v49** When Shaul died, Baal-hanan son of Achbor reigned in his place.

**v50** When Baal-hanan died, Hadad reigned in his place. His city was named Pau, [^22] and his wife's name was Mehetabel daughter of Matred, the daughter of Me-zahab.

**v51** Then Hadad died. Now the chiefs of Edom were Timna, Alvah, Jetheth,
**v52** Oholibamah, Elah, Pinon,
**v53** Kenaz, Teman, Mibzar,
**v54** Magdiel, and Iram. These were the chiefs of Edom.
[^1]: LXX; Hebrew does not include The sons of Noah:; see Genesis 5:32. ( 1:4)
[^2]: Many Hebrew manuscripts and Vulgate (see also LXX and Genesis 10:3); most Hebrew manuscripts Diphath ( 1:6)
[^3]: Sabta is a variant of Sabtah; see Genesis 10:7. ( 1:9)
[^4]: Or who established himself as a mighty warrior ( 1:10)
[^5]: Some translators adjust the Hebrew word order to the Casluhites, and the Caphtorites (from whom the Philistines came); see also Jeremiah 47:4 and Amos 9:7. ( 1:12)
[^6]: Or of the Sidonians, the foremost ( 1:13)
[^7]: Hebrew and of Heth ( 1:13)
[^8]: One Hebrew manuscript and some LXX manuscripts (see also Genesis 10:23); most Hebrew manuscripts do not include The sons of Aram. ( 1:17)
[^9]: Meshech is a variant of Mash; see Genesis 10:23. ( 1:17)
[^10]: Peleg means division. ( 1:19)
[^11]: LXX and Syriac (see also Genesis 10:28); Hebrew Ebal ( 1:22)
[^12]: Literally Shem, Arphaxad, Shelah; some LXX manuscripts Shem, Arphaxad, Cainan, Shelah; see also Genesis 10:24 LXX and Luke 3:35–36. ( 1:24)
[^13]: Many Hebrew manuscripts, some LXX manuscripts, and Syriac (see also Genesis 36:11); most Hebrew manuscripts Zephi ( 1:36)
[^14]: Homam is a variant of Hemam; see Genesis 36:22. ( 1:39)
[^15]: LXX (see also Genesis 36:23); Hebrew Alian ( 1:40)
[^16]: LXX (see also Genesis 36:23); Hebrew Shephi ( 1:40)
[^17]: Hebrew sons ( 1:41)
[^18]: LXX (see also Genesis 36:26); Hebrew Hamran ( 1:41)
[^19]: LXX (see also Genesis 36:27); Hebrew Jaakan ( 1:42)
[^20]: Or before an Israelite king ruled over them ( 1:43)
[^21]: Hebrew the River ( 1:48)
[^22]: Many MT manuscripts, some LXX manuscripts, Vulgate, and Syriac (see also Genesis 36:39); most MT manuscripts Pai ( 1:50)

### Chapter 2

#### The Sons of Israel
**v1** These were the sons of Israel:Reuben, Simeon, Levi, Judah, Issachar, Zebulun,
**v2** Dan, Joseph, Benjamin, Naphtali, Gad, and Asher.

**v3** The sons of Judah:Er, Onan, and Shelah. These three were born to him by Bath-shua the Canaanite. Er, Judah's firstborn, was wicked in the sight of the LORD. So the LORD put him to death.

**v4** Tamar, Judah's daughter-in-law, bore to him Perez and Zerah. Judah had five sons in all.

**v5** The sons of Perez:Hezron and Hamul.

**v6** The sons of Zerah:Zimri, [^1] Ethan, Heman, Calcol, and Dara [^2]—five in all.

**v7** The son [^3] of Carmi:Achar, [^4] who brought trouble upon Israel by violating the ban on devoted things.

**v8** The son of Ethan:Azariah.

**v9** The sons who were born to Hezron:Jerahmeel, Ram, and Caleb. [^5]

**v10** Ram was the father of Amminadab, and Amminadab was the father of Nahshon, a leader of the descendants of Judah.

**v11** Nahshon was the father of Salmon, [^6] and Salmon was the father of Boaz.

**v12** Boaz was the father of Obed, and Obed was the father of Jesse.

**v13** Jesse was the father of Eliab his firstborn; Abinadab was born second, Shimea [^7] third,
**v14** Nethanel fourth, Raddai fifth,
**v15** Ozem sixth, and David seventh.
**v16** Their sisters were Zeruiah and Abigail. And the three sons of Zeruiah were Abishai, Joab, and Asahel.
**v17** Abigail was the mother of Amasa, whose father was Jether [^8] the Ishmaelite.

**v18** Caleb son of Hezron had children by his wife Azubah and by Jerioth. These were the sons of Azubah: Jesher, Shobab, and Ardon.
**v19** When Azubah died, Caleb married Ephrath, [^9] who bore to him Hur.
**v20** Hur was the father of Uri, and Uri was the father of Bezalel.

**v21** Later, Hezron slept with the daughter of Machir the father of Gilead. He had married her when he was sixty years old, and she bore to him Segub.
**v22** Segub was the father of Jair, who had twenty-three cities in the land of Gilead.
**v23** But Geshur and Aram captured Havvoth-jair, [^10] along with Kenath and its sixty surrounding villages. All these were descendants of Machir the father of Gilead.

**v24** After Hezron died in Caleb-ephrathah, his wife Abijah bore [^11] to him Ashhur the father [^12] of Tekoa.

**v25** The sons of Jerahmeel the firstborn of Hezron:Ram his firstborn, Bunah, Oren, Ozem, and Ahijah.
**v26** Jerahmeel had another wife named Atarah, who was the mother of Onam.

**v27** The sons of Ram the firstborn of Jerahmeel:Maaz, Jamin, and Eker.

**v28** The sons of Onam:Shammai and Jada. The sons of Shammai:Nadab and Abishur.
**v29** Abishur's wife was named Abihail, and she bore to him Ahban and Molid.

**v30** The sons of Nadab:Seled and Appaim. Seled died without children.

**v31** The son [^13] of Appaim:Ishi. The son of Ishi:Sheshan. The son of Sheshan:Ahlai.

**v32** The sons of Jada the brother of Shammai:Jether and Jonathan. Jether died without children.

**v33** The sons of Jonathan:Peleth and Zaza. These were the descendants of Jerahmeel.

**v34** Sheshan had no sons, but only daughters. He also had an Egyptian servant named Jarha.
**v35** Sheshan gave his daughter in marriage to his servant Jarha, and she bore to him Attai.

**v36** Attai was the father of Nathan, Nathan was the father of Zabad,
**v37** Zabad was the father of Ephlal, Ephlal was the father of Obed,
**v38** Obed was the father of Jehu, Jehu was the father of Azariah,
**v39** Azariah was the father of Helez, Helez was the father of Elasah,
**v40** Elasah was the father of Sismai, Sismai was the father of Shallum,
**v41** Shallum was the father of Jekamiah, and Jekamiah was the father of Elishama.

**v42** The sons of Caleb the brother of Jerahmeel:Mesha [^14] his firstborn, who was the father of Ziph, and Mareshah his second son, who was the father of Hebron.

**v43** The sons of Hebron:Korah, Tappuah, Rekem, and Shema.
**v44** Shema was the father of Raham the father of Jorkeam, and Rekem was the father of Shammai.
**v45** The son of Shammai was Maon, and Maon was the father of Beth-zur.

**v46** Caleb's concubine Ephah was the mother of Haran, Moza, and Gazez. Haran was the father of Gazez.

**v47** The sons of Jahdai:Regem, Jotham, Geshan, Pelet, Ephah, and Shaaph.

**v48** Caleb's concubine Maacah was the mother of Sheber and Tirhanah.
**v49** She was also the mother of Shaaph father of Madmannah, and of Sheva father of Machbenah and Gibea. Caleb's daughter was Acsah.
**v50** These were the descendants of Caleb. The sons [^15] of Hur the firstborn of Ephrathah: [^16] Shobal the father of Kiriath-jearim,
**v51** Salma the father of Bethlehem, and Hareph the father of Beth-gader.

**v52** These were the descendants of Shobal the father of Kiriath-jearim:Haroeh, half the Manahathites,
**v53** and the clans of Kiriath-jearim—the Ithrites, Puthites, Shumathites, and Mishraites. From these descended the Zorathites and Eshtaolites.

**v54** The descendants of Salma:Bethlehem, the Netophathites, Atroth-beth-joab, half the Manahathites, the Zorites,
**v55** and the clans of the scribes [^17] who lived at Jabez—the Tirathites, Shimeathites, and Sucathites. These are the Kenites who came from Hammath, the father of the house of Rechab. [^18]
[^1]: Zimri is a variant of Zabdi; see Joshua 7:1. ( 2:6)
[^2]: Most Hebrew manuscripts; many Hebrew manuscripts, some LXX manuscripts, and Syriac (see also 1 Kings 4:31) Darda ( 2:6)
[^3]: Hebrew sons ( 2:7)
[^4]: Achar means troubler; also called Achan in Joshua 7 and Joshua 22. ( 2:7)
[^5]: Hebrew Chelubai, a variant of Caleb; see verse 18. ( 2:9)
[^6]: LXX (see also Ruth 4:20–21); Hebrew Salma; twice in this verse ( 2:11)
[^7]: Shimea is a variant of Shammah, Shimeah, and Shimei; see 1 Samuel 16:9, 2 Samuel 13:3, and 2 Samuel 21:21. ( 2:13)
[^8]: Jether is a variant of Ithra; see 2 Samuel 17:25. ( 2:17)
[^9]: Ephrath is a variant of Ephrathah; see verse 50. ( 2:19)
[^10]: Or the villages of Jair ( 2:23)
[^11]: Or After Hezron died, Caleb had relations with Ephrathah, the wife of Hezron his father, and she bore; see also LXX. ( 2:24)
[^12]: Or the founder; also in verses 42, 45, 49, and possibly elsewhere ( 2:24)
[^13]: Hebrew sons; three times in this verse ( 2:31)
[^14]: Hebrew; LXX Mareshah ( 2:42)
[^15]: LXX and Vulgate; Hebrew son ( 2:50)
[^16]: Ephrathah is a variant of Ephrath; see verse 19. ( 2:50)
[^17]: Or of the Sopherites ( 2:55)
[^18]: Or the father of Beth-rechab or the founder of the house of Rechab ( 2:55)

### Chapter 3

#### The Descendants of David
**v1** These were the sons of David who were born to him in Hebron:The firstborn was Amnon by Ahinoam of Jezreel; the second was Daniel by Abigail of Carmel;

**v2** the third was Absalom the son of Maacah daughter of King Talmai of Geshur; the fourth was Adonijah the son of Haggith;

**v3** the fifth was Shephatiah by Abital; and the sixth was Ithream by his wife Eglah.

**v4** These six sons were born to David in Hebron, where he reigned seven years and six months. And David reigned in Jerusalem thirty-three years,
**v5** and these sons were born to him in Jerusalem:Shimea, [^1] Shobab, Nathan, and Solomon. These four were born to him by Bathsheba [^2] daughter of Ammiel. [^3]

**v6** David's other sons were Ibhar, [^4] Elishua, [^5] Eliphelet,
**v7** Nogah, Nepheg, Japhia,
**v8** Elishama, Eliada, and Eliphelet—nine in all.

**v9** These were all the sons of David, besides the sons by his concubines. And Tamar was their sister.
#### The Descendants of Solomon

**v10** Solomon's son was Rehoboam:Abijah was his son, Asa his son, Jehoshaphat his son,
**v11** Joram [^6] his son, Ahaziah his son, Joash his son,
**v12** Amaziah his son, Azariah [^7] his son, Jotham his son,
**v13** Ahaz his son, Hezekiah his son, Manasseh his son,
**v14** Amon his son, and Josiah his son.

**v15** The sons of Josiah:Johanan was the firstborn, Jehoiakim the second, Zedekiah the third, and Shallum [^8] the fourth.

**v16** The successors of Jehoiakim:Jeconiah [^9] his son, and Zedekiah.
#### The Royal Line After the Exile

**v17** The descendants of Jeconiah the captive:Shealtiel his son,
**v18** Malchiram, Pedaiah, Shenazzar, Jekamiah, Hoshama, and Nedabiah.

**v19** The sons of Pedaiah:Zerubbabel and Shimei. The children of Zerubbabel:Meshullam and Hananiah, their sister Shelomith,
**v20** and five others: Hashubah, Ohel, Berechiah, Hasadiah, and Jushab-hesed.

**v21** The descendants of Hananiah:Pelatiah, Jeshaiah, and the sons of Rephaiah, of Arnan, of Obadiah, and of Shecaniah.

**v22** The six descendants of Shecaniah were Shemaiah and his sons:Hattush, Igal, Bariah, Neariah, and Shaphat.

**v23** The sons of Neariah:Elioenai, Hizkiah, and Azrikam—three in all.

**v24** The sons of Elioenai:Hodaviah, Eliashib, Pelaiah, Akkub, Johanan, Delaiah, and Anani—seven in all.
[^1]: Shimea is a variant of Shammua; see 2 Samuel 5:14 and 1 Chronicles 14:4. ( 3:5)
[^2]: One Hebrew manuscript and Vulgate (see also LXX and 2 Samuel 11:3); most Hebrew manuscripts Bath-shua ( 3:5)
[^3]: Ammiel is a variant of Eliam; see 2 Samuel 11:3. ( 3:5)
[^4]: Hebrew does not include David's other sons were. ( 3:6)
[^5]: Two Hebrew manuscripts (see also 2 Samuel 5:15 and 1 Chronicles 14:5); most Hebrew manuscripts Elishama ( 3:6)
[^6]: Joram is a variant spelling of Jehoram. ( 3:11)
[^7]: Azariah is also called Uzziah; see 2 Chronicles 26:1. ( 3:12)
[^8]: Shallum is another name for Jehoahaz. ( 3:15)
[^9]: Jeconiah is a variant of Jehoiachin; also in verse 17; see 2 Kings 24:6. ( 3:16)

### Chapter 4

#### The Descendants of Judah
**v1** The descendants of Judah:Perez, Hezron, Carmi, Hur, and Shobal.

**v2** Reaiah son of Shobal was the father of Jahath, and Jahath was the father of Ahumai and Lahad. These were the clans of the Zorathites.

**v3** These were the sons [^1] of Etam: Jezreel, Ishma, and Idbash. And their sister was named Hazzelelponi.
**v4** Penuel was the father [^2] of Gedor, and Ezer was the father of Hushah. These were the descendants of Hur, the firstborn of Ephrathah and the father of Bethlehem.

**v5** Ashhur the father of Tekoa had two wives, Helah and Naarah.

**v6** Naarah bore to him Ahuzzam, Hepher, Temeni, and Haahashtari. These were the descendants of Naarah.

**v7** The sons of Helah were Zereth, Zohar, [^3] Ethnan,
**v8** and Koz, who was the father of Anub and Zobebah and of the clans of Aharhel son of Harum.
#### The Prayer of Jabez

**v9** Now Jabez was more honorable than his brothers. His mother had named him Jabez, [^4] saying, "Because I bore him in pain."

**v10** And Jabez called out to the God of Israel, "If only You would bless me and enlarge my territory! May Your hand be with me and keep me from harm, so that I will be free from pain." And God granted the request of Jabez.
#### More Descendants of Judah

**v11** Chelub the brother of Shuhah was the father of Mehir, who was the father of Eshton.
**v12** Eshton was the father of Beth-rapha, of Paseah, and of Tehinnah the father of Ir-nahash. These were the men of Recah.

**v13** The sons of Kenaz:Othniel and Seraiah. The sons of Othniel:Hathath and Meonothai. [^5]

**v14** Meonothai was the father of Ophrah, and Seraiah was the father of Joab, the father of those living in Ge-harashim, which was given this name because its people were craftsmen. [^6]

**v15** The sons of Caleb son of Jephunneh:Iru, Elah, and Naam. The son [^7] of Elah:Kenaz.

**v16** The sons of Jehallelel:Ziph, Ziphah, Tiria, and Asarel.

**v17** The sons of Ezrah:Jether, Mered, Epher, and Jalon. And Mered's wife Bithiah gave birth [^8] to Miriam, Shammai, and Ishbah the father of Eshtemoa.
**v18** These were the children of Pharaoh's daughter Bithiah. [^9] Mered also took a Judean [^10] wife, who gave birth to Jered the father of Gedor, Heber the father of Soco, and Jekuthiel the father of Zanoah.

**v19** The sons of Hodiah's wife, the sister of Naham, were the fathers of Keilah the Garmite and of Eshtemoa the Maacathite. [^11]

**v20** The sons of Shimon:Amnon, Rinnah, Ben-hanan, and Tilon. The descendants of Ishi:Zoheth and Ben-zoheth.

**v21** The sons of Shelah son of Judah:Er the father of Lecah, Laadah the father of Mareshah and the clans of the linen workers at Beth-ashbea,
**v22** Jokim, the men of Cozeba, and Joash and Saraph, who ruled in Moab and Jashubi-lehem. (These names are from ancient records.)
**v23** These were the potters who lived at Netaim and Gederah. They lived there in the service of the king.
#### The Descendants of Simeon

**v24** The descendants of Simeon:Nemuel, Jamin, Jarib, Zerah, [^12] and Shaul.

**v25** The sons of Shaul:Shallum, Mibsam, and Mishma.

**v26** The sons of Mishma:Hammuel, Zaccur, and Shimei.

**v27** Shimei had sixteen sons and six daughters, but his brothers did not have many children, so their whole clan did not become as numerous as the sons of Judah.
**v28** They lived in Beersheba, Moladah, Hazar-shual,
**v29** Bilhah, Ezem, Tolad,
**v30** Bethuel, Hormah, Ziklag,
**v31** Beth-marcaboth, Hazar-susim, Beth-biri, and Shaaraim. These were their cities until the reign of David.
**v32** And their villages were Etam, Ain, Rimmon, Tochen, and Ashan—five towns—
**v33** and all their surrounding villages as far as Baal. [^13] These were their settlements, and they kept a genealogical record:

**v34** Meshobab, Jamlech, Joshah son of Amaziah,
**v35** Joel, Jehu son of Joshibiah (son of Seraiah, son of Asiel),
**v36** Elioenai, Jaakobah, Jeshohaiah, Asaiah, Adiel, Jesimiel, Benaiah,
**v37** and Ziza son of Shiphi (son of Allon, son of Jedaiah, son of Shimri, son of Shemaiah).

**v38** These men listed by name were the leaders of their clans. Their families increased greatly,
**v39** and they journeyed to the entrance of Gedor, to the east side of the valley, in search of pasture for their flocks.
**v40** There they found rich, good pasture, and the land was spacious, peaceful, and quiet; for some Hamites had lived there formerly.

**v41** These who were noted by name came in the days of Hezekiah king of Judah. They attacked the Hamites in their dwellings as well as the Meunites who were there, devoting them to destruction [^14] even to this day. Then they settled in their place, because there was pasture for their flocks.
**v42** And five hundred of these Simeonites led by Pelatiah, Neariah, Rephaiah, and Uzziel, the sons of Ishi, went to Mount Seir
**v43** and struck down the remnant of the Amalekites who had escaped. And they have lived there to this day.
[^1]: LXX (see also Vulgate); Hebrew These were of the father ( 4:3)
[^2]: Or the founder; also in verses 5, 12, 14, 17, 18, and possibly elsewhere ( 4:4)
[^3]: Alternate MT reading; the other alternate (see also Vulgate) reads Izhar. ( 4:7)
[^4]: Jabez sounds like the Hebrew for pain or distress. ( 4:9)
[^5]: Vulgate and some LXX; Hebrew does not include and Meonothai. ( 4:13)
[^6]: Literally Ge-harashim, for they were craftsmen. The Hebrew Ge-harashim means valley of craftsmen. ( 4:14)
[^7]: Hebrew sons ( 4:15)
[^8]: Literally she gave birth ( 4:17)
[^9]: This statement is at the end of verse 18 in the Hebrew. ( 4:18)
[^10]: Or Judahite ( 4:18)
[^11]: Or were the father of Keilah the Garmite, and Eshtemoa the Maacathite ( 4:19)
[^12]: Zerah is a variant of Zohar; see Genesis 46:10 and Exodus 6:15. ( 4:24)
[^13]: Hebrew; some LXX manuscripts (see also Joshua 19:8) Baalath ( 4:33)
[^14]: Forms of the Hebrew cherem refer to the giving over of things or persons to the LORD, either by destroying them or by giving them as an offering. ( 4:41)

### Chapter 5

#### The Descendants of Reuben
**v1** These were the sons of Reuben the firstborn of Israel. Though he was the firstborn, his birthright was given to the sons of Joseph son of Israel, because Reuben defiled his father's bed. So he is not reckoned according to birthright.
**v2** And though Judah prevailed over his brothers and a ruler came from him, the birthright belonged to Joseph.
**v3** The sons of Reuben, the firstborn of Israel:Hanoch, Pallu, Hezron, and Carmi.

**v4** The descendants of Joel:Shemaiah his son, Gog his son, Shimei his son,
**v5** Micah his son, Reaiah his son, Baal his son,
**v6** and Beerah his son, whom Tiglath-pileser [^1] king of Assyria carried into exile. Beerah was a leader of the Reubenites.
**v7** His relatives by their clans are recorded in their genealogy:Jeiel the chief, Zechariah,
**v8** and Bela son of Azaz, the son of Shema, the son of Joel. They settled in Aroer and as far as Nebo and Baal-meon.
**v9** They also settled in the east as far as the edge of the desert that extends to the Euphrates River, because their livestock had increased in the land of Gilead.

**v10** During the days of Saul they waged war against the Hagrites, who were defeated at their hands, and they occupied the dwellings of the Hagrites throughout the region east of Gilead.
#### The Descendants of Gad

**v11** The descendants of Gad lived next to the Reubenites in the land of Bashan, as far as Salecah:

**v12** Joel was the chief, Shapham the second, then Jaanai and Shaphat, who lived in Bashan.

**v13** Their kinsmen by families were Michael, Meshullam, Sheba, Jorai, Jacan, Zia, and Eber—seven in all.
**v14** These were the sons of Abihail son of Huri, the son of Jaroah, the son of Gilead, the son of Michael, the son of Jeshishai, the son of Jahdo, the son of Buz.
**v15** Ahi son of Abdiel, the son of Guni, was head of their family.

**v16** They lived in Gilead, in Bashan and its towns, and throughout the pasturelands of Sharon.
**v17** All of them were recorded in the genealogies during the reigns of Jotham king of Judah and Jeroboam king of Israel.

**v18** The Reubenites, the Gadites, and the half-tribe of Manasseh had 44,760 warriors—valiant men who carried the shield and sword, drew the bow, and were trained for battle.
**v19** They waged war against the Hagrites, as well as Jetur, Naphish, and Nodab.

**v20** And because they cried out to God in battle, they were helped against their enemies, and the Hagrites and all their allies were delivered into their hands. Because they put their trust in God, He answered their prayers.
**v21** They seized the livestock of the Hagrites—50,000 camels, 250,000 sheep, and 2,000 donkeys. They also took 100,000 captives,
**v22** and many others fell slain, because the battle belonged to God. And they occupied the land until the exile.
#### The Half-Tribe of Manasseh

**v23** Now the people of the half-tribe of Manasseh were numerous. They settled in the land from Bashan to Baal-hermon (that is, Senir, also known as Mount Hermon). [^2]
**v24** These were the heads of their families:Epher, Ishi, Eliel, Azriel, Jeremiah, Hodaviah, and Jahdiel. They were mighty men of valor, famous men, and heads of their families.
**v25** But they were unfaithful to the God of their fathers, and they prostituted themselves with the gods of the peoples of the land, whom God had destroyed before them.

**v26** So the God of Israel stirred up the spirit of Pul king of Assyria (that is, Tiglath-pileser king of Assyria) to take the Reubenites, the Gadites, and the half-tribe of Manasseh into exile. And he brought them to Halah, Habor, Hara, and the river of Gozan, where they remain to this day.
[^1]: Hebrew Tilgath-pilneser, a variant spelling of Tiglath-pileser; also in verse 26 ( 5:6)
[^2]: Literally from Bashan to Baal-hermon and Senir and Mount Hermon. ( 5:23)

### Chapter 6

#### The Descendants of Levi
**v1** The sons of Levi:Gershon, Kohath, and Merari.

**v2** The sons of Kohath:Amram, Izhar, Hebron, and Uzziel.

**v3** The children of Amram:Aaron, Moses, and Miriam. The sons of Aaron:Nadab, Abihu, Eleazar, and Ithamar.

**v4** Eleazar was the father of Phinehas, Phinehas was the father of Abishua,

**v5** Abishua was the father of Bukki, Bukki was the father of Uzzi,

**v6** Uzzi was the father of Zerahiah, Zerahiah was the father of Meraioth,

**v7** Meraioth was the father of Amariah, Amariah was the father of Ahitub,

**v8** Ahitub was the father of Zadok, Zadok was the father of Ahimaaz,

**v9** Ahimaaz was the father of Azariah, Azariah was the father of Johanan,

**v10** Johanan was the father of Azariah, who served as priest in the temple that Solomon built in Jerusalem,

**v11** Azariah was the father of Amariah, Amariah was the father of Ahitub,

**v12** Ahitub was the father of Zadok, Zadok was the father of Shallum,

**v13** Shallum was the father of Hilkiah, Hilkiah was the father of Azariah,

**v14** Azariah was the father of Seraiah, and Seraiah was the father of Jehozadak. [^1]

**v15** Jehozadak went into captivity when the LORD sent Judah and Jerusalem into exile by the hand of Nebuchadnezzar.

**v16** The sons of Levi:Gershom, [^2] Kohath, and Merari.

**v17** These are the names of the sons of Gershom:Libni and Shimei.

**v18** The sons of Kohath:Amram, Izhar, Hebron, and Uzziel.

**v19** The sons of Merari:Mahli and Mushi. These are the clans of the Levites listed according to their fathers:

**v20** Of Gershom:Libni his son, Jahath his son, Zimmah his son,
**v21** Joah his son, Iddo his son, Zerah his son, and Jeatherai his son.

**v22** The descendants of Kohath:Amminadab his son, Korah his son, Assir his son,
**v23** Elkanah his son, Ebiasaph [^3] his son, Assir his son,
**v24** Tahath his son, Uriel his son, Uzziah his son, and Shaul his son.

**v25** The descendants of Elkanah:Amasai, Ahimoth,
**v26** Elkanah his son, [^4] Zophai his son, Nahath his son,
**v27** Eliab [^5] his son, Jeroham his son, and Elkanah his son. [^6]

**v28** The sons of Samuel:Joel his firstborn and Abijah his second son. [^7]

**v29** The descendants of Merari:Mahli, Libni his son, Shimei his son, Uzzah his son,
**v30** Shimea his son, Haggiah his son, and Asaiah his son.
#### The Temple Musicians

**v31** These are the men David put in charge of the music in the house of the LORD after the ark rested there.
**v32** They ministered with song before the tabernacle, the Tent of Meeting, until Solomon built the house of the LORD in Jerusalem. And they performed their duties according to the regulations given them.
**v33** These are the men who served, together with their sons. From the Kohathites:Heman the singer, the son of Joel, the son of Samuel,
**v34** the son of Elkanah, the son of Jeroham, the son of Eliel, [^8] the son of Toah,
**v35** the son of Zuph, the son of Elkanah, the son of Mahath, the son of Amasai,
**v36** the son of Elkanah, the son of Joel, the son of Azariah, the son of Zephaniah,
**v37** the son of Tahath, the son of Assir, the son of Ebiasaph, the son of Korah,
**v38** the son of Izhar, the son of Kohath, the son of Levi, the son of Israel.

**v39** Heman's kinsman was Asaph, who served at his right hand:Asaph the son of Berechiah, the son of Shimea,
**v40** the son of Michael, the son of Baaseiah, [^9] the son of Malchijah,
**v41** the son of Ethni, the son of Zerah, the son of Adaiah,
**v42** the son of Ethan, the son of Zimmah, the son of Shimei,
**v43** the son of Jahath, the son of Gershom, the son of Levi.

**v44** On the left were their kinsmen, the sons of Merari:Ethan the son of Kishi, the son of Abdi, the son of Malluch,
**v45** the son of Hashabiah, the son of Amaziah, the son of Hilkiah,
**v46** the son of Amzi, the son of Bani, the son of Shemer,
**v47** the son of Mahli, the son of Mushi, the son of Merari, the son of Levi.
#### The Descendants of Aaron

**v48** Their fellow Levites were assigned to every kind of service of the tabernacle, the house of God.
**v49** But Aaron and his sons did all the work of the Most Holy Place. [^10] They presented the offerings on the altar of burnt offering and on the altar of incense to make atonement for Israel, according to all that Moses the servant of God had commanded.

**v50** These were the descendants of Aaron:Eleazar his son, Phinehas his son, Abishua his son,
**v51** Bukki his son, Uzzi his son, Zerahiah his son,
**v52** Meraioth his son, Amariah his son, Ahitub his son,
**v53** Zadok his son, and Ahimaaz his son.
#### Territories for the Levites

**v54** Now these were the territories assigned to the descendants of Aaron from the Kohathite clan for their settlements, because the first lot fell to them:

**v55** They were given Hebron in the land of Judah and its surrounding pasturelands.
**v56** But the fields and villages around the city were given to Caleb son of Jephunneh.
**v57** So the descendants of Aaron were given Hebron (a city of refuge), Libnah, [^11] Jattir, Eshtemoa,
**v58** Hilen, [^12] Debir,
**v59** Ashan, [^13] Juttah, [^14] and Beth-shemesh, together with their pasturelands.

**v60** And from the tribe of Benjamin they were given Gibeon, [^15] Geba, Alemeth, and Anathoth, together with their pasturelands. So they had thirteen cities in all among their families.

**v61** To the rest of the Kohathites, ten cities were allotted from the half-tribe of Manasseh.

**v62** The Gershomites, [^16] according to their clans, were allotted thirteen cities from the tribes of Issachar, Asher, Naphtali, and Manasseh in Bashan.

**v63** The Merarites, according to their clans, were allotted twelve cities from the tribes of Reuben, Gad, and Zebulun.

**v64** So the Israelites gave to the Levites these cities and their pasturelands.
**v65** They assigned by lot the cities named above from the tribes of Judah, Simeon, and Benjamin.

**v66** And some of the clans of the Kohathites were given cities from the tribe of Ephraim for their territory:

**v67** They were given Shechem (a city of refuge)[^17] with its pasturelands in the hill country of Ephraim, and Gezer,
**v68** Jokmeam, Beth-horon,
**v69** Aijalon, and Gath-rimmon, together with their pasturelands.

**v70** And from the half-tribe of Manasseh the rest of the clans of the Kohathites were given Aner and Bileam, together with their pasturelands.

**v71** The Gershomites received the following:From the clan of the half-tribe of Manasseh they were given Golan in Bashan and also Ashtaroth, together with their pasturelands.

**v72** From the tribe of Issachar they were given Kedesh, Daberath,
**v73** Ramoth, and Anem, together with their pasturelands.

**v74** From the tribe of Asher they were given Mashal, Abdon,
**v75** Hukok, and Rehob, together with their pasturelands.

**v76** And from the tribe of Naphtali they were given Kedesh in Galilee, Hammon, and Kiriathaim, together with their pasturelands.

**v77** The Merarites (the rest of the Levites) received the following:From the tribe of Zebulun they were given Rimmono and Tabor, [^18] together with their pasturelands.

**v78** From the tribe of Reuben east of the Jordan opposite Jericho they were given Bezer in the wilderness, Jahzah, [^19]
**v79** Kedemoth, and Mephaath, together with their pasturelands.

**v80** And from the tribe of Gad they were given Ramoth in Gilead, Mahanaim,
**v81** Heshbon, and Jazer, together with their pasturelands.
[^1]: Jehozadak is a variant of Jozadak; also in verse 15; see Ezra 3:2. ( 6:14)
[^2]: Gershom is a variant of Gershon; similarly in verses 17, 20, 43, 62, and 71; see verse 1. ( 6:16)
[^3]: Ebiasaph is a variant of Abiasaph; also in verse 37; see Exodus 6:24. ( 6:23)
[^4]: Some Hebrew manuscripts, LXX, and Syriac; most Hebrew manuscripts Elkanah. The sons of Elkanah: ( 6:26)
[^5]: Eliab is also called Eliel; see verse 34. Both of these are other names for Elihu; see 1 Samuel 1:1. ( 6:27)
[^6]: Hebrew; some LXX manuscripts include and Samuel his son; see verses 33–34 and 1 Samuel 1:19–20. ( 6:27)
[^7]: See Syriac and some LXX manuscripts (also verse 33 and 1 Samuel 8:2); Hebrew The sons of Samuel: the firstborn Vashni, then Abiah. ( 6:28)
[^8]: Eliel is also called Eliab; see verse 27. Both of these are other names for Elihu; see 1 Samuel 1:1. ( 6:34)
[^9]: Most Hebrew manuscripts; some Hebrew manuscripts, one LXX manuscript, and Syriac Maaseiah ( 6:40)
[^10]: Or the Holy of Holies ( 6:49)
[^11]: As in the parallel text at Joshua 21:13; Hebrew were given the cities of refuge: Hebron, Libnah ( 6:57)
[^12]: Some Hebrew manuscripts; MT Hilez; parallel text at Joshua 21:15 Holon ( 6:58)
[^13]: Ashan is a variant of Ain; see Joshua 21:16. ( 6:59)
[^14]: Syriac and the parallel text at Joshua 21:16; MT does not include Juttah, ( 6:59)
[^15]: LXX, Syriac, and parallel text at Joshua 21:17; MT does not include Gibeon, ( 6:60)
[^16]: Gershomites is a variant of Gershonites; also in verse 71; see 1 Chronicles 23:7. ( 6:62)
[^17]: As in the parallel text at Joshua 21:21; Hebrew They were given the cities of refuge: Shechem ( 6:67)
[^18]: LXX (they were given) Jokneam, Kartah, Rimmono, and Tabor; see Joshua 21:34. ( 6:77)
[^19]: Jahzah is a variant of Jahaz; see Numbers 21:23. ( 6:78)

### Chapter 7

#### The Descendants of Issachar
**v1** The sons of Issachar:Tola, Puah, [^1] Jashub, and Shimron—four in all.

**v2** The sons of Tola:Uzzi, Rephaiah, Jeriel, Jahmai, Ibsam, and Shemuel, the heads of their families. In the days of David, 22,600 descendants of Tola were numbered in their genealogies as mighty men of valor.

**v3** The son [^2] of Uzzi:Izrahiah. The sons of Izrahiah:Michael, Obadiah, Joel, and Isshiah. All five of them were chiefs.
**v4** In addition to them, according to their genealogy, they had 36,000 troops for battle, for they had many wives and children.

**v5** Their kinsmen belonging to all the families of Issachar who were mighty men of valor totaled 87,000, as listed in their genealogies.
#### The Descendants of Benjamin

**v6** The three sons of Benjamin:Bela, Becher, and Jediael.

**v7** The sons of Bela:Ezbon, Uzzi, Uzziel, Jerimoth, and Iri, heads of their families—five in all. There were 22,034 mighty men of valor listed in their genealogies.

**v8** The sons of Becher:Zemirah, Joash, Eliezer, Elioenai, Omri, Jeremoth, Abijah, Anathoth, and Alemeth; all these were Becher's sons.
**v9** Their genealogies were recorded according to the heads of their families—20,200 mighty men of valor.

**v10** The son of Jediael:Bilhan. The sons of Bilhan:Jeush, Benjamin, Ehud, Chenaanah, Zethan, Tarshish, and Ahishahar.
**v11** All these sons of Jediael were heads of their families, mighty men of valor; there were 17,200 fit for battle.
**v12** The Shuppites and Huppites were descendants of Ir, and the Hushites were descendants of Aher.
#### The Descendants of Naphtali

**v13** The sons of Naphtali:Jahziel, [^3] Guni, Jezer, and Shallum [^4]—the descendants of Bilhah.
#### The Descendants of Manasseh

**v14** The descendants of Manasseh:Asriel through his Aramean concubine. She also gave birth to Machir the father of Gilead.

**v15** Machir took a wife from among the Huppites and Shuppites. The name of his sister was Maacah. Another descendant was named Zelophehad, who had only daughters.

**v16** Machir's wife Maacah gave birth to a son, and she named him Peresh. His brother was named Sheresh, and his sons were Ulam and Rekem.

**v17** The son of Ulam:Bedan. These were the sons of Gilead son of Machir, the son of Manasseh.
**v18** His sister Hammolecheth gave birth to Ishhod, Abiezer, and Mahlah.

**v19** And these were the sons of Shemida:Ahian, Shechem, Likhi, and Aniam.
#### The Descendants of Ephraim

**v20** The descendants of Ephraim:Shuthelah, Bered his son, Tahath his son, Eleadah his son, Tahath his son,
**v21** Zabad his son, and Shuthelah his son. Ezer and Elead were killed by the natives of Gath, because they went down to steal their livestock.

**v22** Their father Ephraim mourned for many days, and his relatives came to comfort him.
**v23** And again he slept with his wife, and she conceived and gave birth to a son. So he named him Beriah, [^5] because tragedy had come upon his house.
**v24** His daughter was Sheerah, who built Lower and Upper Beth-horon, as well as Uzzen-sheerah.

**v25** Additionally, Rephah was his son, Resheph his son, [^6] Telah his son, Tahan his son,
**v26** Ladan his son, Ammihud his son, Elishama his son,
**v27** Nun [^7] his son, and Joshua his son.

**v28** Their holdings and settlements included Bethel and its villages, Naaran to the east, Gezer and its villages to the west, and Shechem and its villages as far as Ayyah [^8] and its villages.
**v29** And along the borders of Manasseh were Beth-shean, [^9] Taanach, Megiddo, and Dor, together with their villages. The descendants of Joseph son of Israel lived in these towns.
#### The Descendants of Asher

**v30** The children of Asher:Imnah, Ishvah, Ishvi, Beriah, and their sister Serah.

**v31** The sons of Beriah:Heber, as well as Malchiel, who was the father of Birzaith.

**v32** Heber was the father of Japhlet, Shomer, and Hotham, and of their sister Shua.

**v33** The sons of Japhlet:Pasach, Bimhal, and Ashvath. These were Japhlet's sons.

**v34** The sons of Shemer:Ahi, Rohgah, [^10] Hubbah, and Aram.

**v35** The sons of his brother Helem: [^11] Zophah, Imna, Shelesh, and Amal.

**v36** The sons of Zophah:Suah, Harnepher, Shual, Beri, Imrah,
**v37** Bezer, Hod, Shamma, Shilshah, Ithran, [^12] and Beera.

**v38** The sons of Jether:Jephunneh, Pispa, and Ara.

**v39** The sons of Ulla:Arah, Hanniel, and Rizia.

**v40** All these were the descendants of Asher—heads of their families, choice and mighty men of valor, and chiefs among the leaders. The number of men fit for battle, recorded in their genealogies, was 26,000.
[^1]: Puah is a variant of Puvah; see Genesis 46:13 and Numbers 26:23. ( 7:1)
[^2]: Hebrew sons; also in verses 10 and 17 ( 7:3)
[^3]: Jahziel is a variant of Jahzeel; see Genesis 46:24. ( 7:13)
[^4]: Most Hebrew manuscripts; some Hebrew and LXX manuscripts Shillem; see Genesis 46:24 and Numbers 26:49. ( 7:13)
[^5]: Beriah sounds like the Hebrew for tragedy or disaster. ( 7:23)
[^6]: Some LXX manuscripts; Hebrew does not include his son. ( 7:25)
[^7]: Or Non ( 7:27)
[^8]: Ayyah is another name for Gaza; see also LXX. ( 7:28)
[^9]: Beth-shean is a variant of Beth-shan; see 1 Samuel 31:10 and 2 Samuel 21:12. ( 7:29)
[^10]: Or The sons of his brother Shemer: Rohgah; note that Shemer is a variant of Shomer; see verse 32. ( 7:34)
[^11]: Helem is possibly another name for Hotham; see verse 32. ( 7:35)
[^12]: Ithran is possibly a variant of Jether; see verse 38. ( 7:37)

### Chapter 8

#### Genealogy from Benjamin to Saul
**v1** Benjamin was the father of Bela his firstborn, Ashbel the second, Aharah the third,
**v2** Nohah the fourth, and Rapha the fifth.

**v3** The sons of Bela:Addar, Gera, Abihud, [^1]
**v4** Abishua, Naaman, Ahoah,
**v5** Gera, Shephuphan, and Huram.

**v6** These were the descendants of Ehud who were the heads of the families living in Geba and were exiled to Manahath:

**v7** Naaman, Ahijah, and Gera, who carried them into exile and who was the father of Uzza and Ahihud. [^2]

**v8** Shaharaim had sons in the country of Moab after he had divorced his wives Hushim and Baara.
**v9** His sons by his wife Hodesh:Jobab, Zibia, Mesha, Malcam,
**v10** Jeuz, Sachia, and Mirmah. These were his sons, heads of families.

**v11** He also had sons by Hushim:Abitub and Elpaal.

**v12** The sons of Elpaal:Eber, Misham, Shemed (who built Ono and Lod with its villages),
**v13** and Beriah and Shema (who were the heads of families living in Aijalon and who drove out the inhabitants of Gath).

**v14** Ahio, Shashak, Jeremoth,
**v15** Zebadiah, Arad, Eder,
**v16** Michael, Ishpah, and Joha were the sons of Beriah.

**v17** Zebadiah, Meshullam, Hizki, Heber,
**v18** Ishmerai, Izliah, and Jobab were the sons of Elpaal.

**v19** Jakim, Zichri, Zabdi,
**v20** Elienai, Zillethai, Eliel,
**v21** Adaiah, Beraiah, and Shimrath were the sons of Shimei.

**v22** Ishpan, Eber, Eliel,
**v23** Abdon, Zichri, Hanan,
**v24** Hananiah, Elam, Anthothijah,
**v25** Iphdeiah, and Penuel were the sons of Shashak.

**v26** Shamsherai, Shehariah, Athaliah,
**v27** Jaareshiah, Elijah, and Zichri were the sons of Jeroham.

**v28** All these were heads of families, the chiefs according to their genealogies, and they lived in Jerusalem.

**v29** Jeiel the father of Gibeon lived [^3] in Gibeon. His wife's name was Maacah,
**v30** and Abdon was his firstborn son, then Zur, Kish, Baal, [^4] Nadab,
**v31** Gedor, Ahio, Zecher, [^5]
**v32** and Mikloth, who was the father of Shimeah. [^6] They too lived alongside their relatives in Jerusalem.
#### The Family of Saul

**v33** Ner was the father of Kish, Kish was the father of Saul, and Saul was the father of Jonathan, Malchishua, Abinadab, and Esh-baal. [^7]

**v34** The son of Jonathan:Merib-baal, [^8] and Merib-baal was the father of Micah. [^9]

**v35** The sons of Micah:Pithon, Melech, Tarea, [^10] and Ahaz.

**v36** Ahaz was the father of Jehoaddah, Jehoaddah [^11] was the father of Alemeth, Azmaveth, and Zimri, and Zimri was the father of Moza.

**v37** Moza was the father of Binea. Raphah [^12] was his son, Eleasah his son, and Azel his son.

**v38** Azel had six sons, and these were their names:Azrikam, Bocheru, Ishmael, Sheariah, Obadiah, and Hanan. All these were the sons of Azel.

**v39** The sons of his brother Eshek:Ulam was his firstborn, Jeush second, and Eliphelet third.
**v40** The sons of Ulam were mighty men of valor, archers, and they had many sons and grandsons—150 in all. All these were the descendants of Benjamin.
[^1]: Possibly Gera the father of Ehud; see verse 7. ( 8:3)
[^2]: Or and Gera, that is Heglam, who was the father of Uzza and Ahihud. ( 8:7)
[^3]: Some LXX manuscripts (see also 1 Chronicles 9:35); Hebrew The father of Gibeon lived ( 8:29)
[^4]: Some LXX manuscripts include Ner; see 1 Chronicles 9:36. ( 8:30)
[^5]: Zecher is likely a variant of Zechariah; see 1 Chronicles 9:37. ( 8:31)
[^6]: Shimeah is a variant of Shimeam; see 1 Chronicles 9:38. ( 8:32)
[^7]: Esh-baal is also called Ish-bosheth; see 2 Samuel 2:8. ( 8:33)
[^8]: Merib-baal is also called Mephibosheth; see 2 Samuel 4:4. ( 8:34)
[^9]: Micah is a variant of Mica; see 2 Samuel 9:12. ( 8:34)
[^10]: Tarea is a variant of Tahrea; see 1 Chronicles 9:41. ( 8:35)
[^11]: Jehoaddah is a variant of Jarah or Jadah; see 1 Chronicles 9:42. ( 8:36)
[^12]: Raphah is a variant of Rephaiah; see 1 Chronicles 9:43. ( 8:37)

### Chapter 9

#### The People of Jerusalem
**v1** So all Israel was recorded in the genealogies written in the Book of the Kings of Israel. But Judah was exiled to Babylon because of their unfaithfulness.

**v2** Now the first to resettle their own property in their cities were Israelites, priests, Levites, and temple servants. [^1]

**v3** Some of the descendants of Judah, Benjamin, Ephraim, and Manasseh lived in Jerusalem:

**v4** Uthai son of Ammihud, the son of Omri, the son of Imri, the son of Bani, a descendant of Perez son of Judah.

**v5** From the Shilonites:Asaiah the firstborn and his sons.

**v6** From the Zerahites:Jeuel and 690 relatives.

**v7** From the Benjamites:Sallu son of Meshullam, the son of Hodaviah, the son of Hassenuah;

**v8** Ibneiah son of Jeroham; Elah son of Uzzi, the son of Michri; Meshullam son of Shephatiah, the son of Reuel, the son of Ibnijah;

**v9** and 956 of their relatives according to their genealogy. All these men were heads of their families.

**v10** From the priests:Jedaiah, Jehoiarib, and Jachin;

**v11** Azariah son of Hilkiah, the son of Meshullam, the son of Zadok, the son of Meraioth, the son of Ahitub, the chief official of God's temple;

**v12** Adaiah son of Jeroham, the son of Pashhur, the son of Malchijah; Maasai son of Adiel, the son of Jahzerah, the son of Meshullam, the son of Meshillemith, [^2] the son of Immer;

**v13** and 1,760 of their relatives, the heads of their families, able men for the work of the service of the house of God.

**v14** From the Levites:Shemaiah son of Hasshub, the son of Azrikam, the son of Hashabiah, a descendant of Merari;

**v15** Bakbakkar, Heresh, Galal, and Mattaniah son of Mica, the son of Zichri, the son of Asaph;

**v16** Obadiah son of Shemaiah, the son of Galal, the son of Jeduthun; and Berechiah son of Asa, the son of Elkanah, who lived in the villages of the Netophathites.

**v17** These were the gatekeepers:Shallum, Akkub, Talmon, Ahiman, and their relatives. Shallum was their chief;
**v18** he was previously stationed at the King's Gate on the east side. These were the gatekeepers from the camp of the Levites.
**v19** Shallum son of Kore, the son of Ebiasaph, [^3] the son of Korah, and his relatives from the Korahites were assigned to guard the thresholds of the Tent, just as their fathers had been assigned to guard the entrance to the dwelling of the LORD.

**v20** In earlier times Phinehas son of Eleazar had been in charge of the gatekeepers, and the LORD was with him.

**v21** Zechariah son of Meshelemiah was the gatekeeper at the entrance to the Tent of Meeting.

**v22** The number of those chosen to be gatekeepers at the thresholds was 212. They were registered by genealogy in their villages. David and Samuel the seer had appointed them to their positions of trust.

**v23** So they and their descendants were assigned to guard the gates of the house of the LORD—the house called the Tent.
**v24** The gatekeepers were stationed on the four sides: east, west, north, and south.
**v25** Their relatives came from their villages at fixed times to serve with them for seven-day periods.
**v26** But the four chief gatekeepers, who were Levites, were entrusted with the rooms and the treasuries of the house of God.
**v27** They would spend the night stationed around the house of God, because they were responsible for guarding it and opening it every morning.

**v28** Some of them were in charge of the articles used in worship, to count them whenever they were brought in or taken out.
**v29** Others were put in charge of the furnishings and other articles of the sanctuary, as well as the fine flour, wine, oil, frankincense, and spices.
**v30** And some of the sons of the priests mixed the spices.

**v31** A Levite named Mattithiah, the firstborn son of Shallum the Korahite, was entrusted with baking the bread.
**v32** Some of their Kohathite relatives were responsible for preparing the rows of the showbread every Sabbath.

**v33** Those who were musicians, the heads of Levite families, stayed in the temple chambers and were exempt from other duties because they were on duty day and night.
**v34** All these were heads of Levite families, chiefs according to their genealogies, and they lived in Jerusalem.
#### The Descendants of Saul

**v35** Jeiel the father [^4] of Gibeon lived in Gibeon. His wife's name was Maacah.

**v36** Abdon was his firstborn son, then Zur, Kish, Baal, Ner, Nadab,
**v37** Gedor, Ahio, Zechariah, and Mikloth.

**v38** Mikloth was the father of Shimeam. [^5] They too lived alongside their relatives in Jerusalem.

**v39** Ner was the father of Kish, Kish was the father of Saul, and Saul was the father of Jonathan, Malchishua, Abinadab, and Esh-baal.

**v40** The son of Jonathan:Merib-baal, who was the father of Micah.

**v41** The sons of Micah:Pithon, Melech, Tahrea, and Ahaz. [^6]

**v42** Ahaz was the father of Jarah; Jarah [^7] was the father of Alemeth, Azmaveth, and Zimri; and Zimri was the father of Moza.
**v43** Moza was the father of Binea. Rephaiah [^8] was his son, Elasah his son, and Azel his son.

**v44** And Azel had six sons, and these were their names:Azrikam, Bocheru, Ishmael, Sheariah, Obadiah, and Hanan. These were the sons of Azel.
[^1]: Hebrew Nethinim ( 9:2)
[^2]: Meshillemith is a variant of Meshillemoth; see Nehemiah 11:13. ( 9:12)
[^3]: Ebiasaph is a variant of Abiasaph; see Exodus 6:24. ( 9:19)
[^4]: Or the founder ( 9:35)
[^5]: Shimeam is a variant of Shimeah; see 1 Chronicles 8:32. ( 9:38)
[^6]: Vulgate and Syriac (see also LXX and 1 Chronicles 8:35); Hebrew Pithon, Melech, and Tahrea; note that Tahrea is a variant of Tarea. ( 9:41)
[^7]: Most Hebrew manuscripts; some Hebrew manuscripts and LXX Jadah; Jadah; note that Jarah and Jadah are variants of Jehoaddah; see 1 Chronicles 8:36. ( 9:42)
[^8]: Rephaiah is a variant of Raphah; see 1 Chronicles 8:37. ( 9:43)

### Chapter 10

#### Saul's Overthrow and Death
**v1** Now the Philistines fought against Israel, and the men of Israel fled before them, and many fell slain on Mount Gilboa.

**v2** The Philistines followed hard after Saul and his sons, and they killed Saul's sons Jonathan, Abinadab, and Malchishua.
**v3** When the battle intensified against Saul, the archers overtook him and wounded him.

**v4** Then Saul said to his armor-bearer, "Draw your sword and run me through with it, or these uncircumcised men will come and torture me!" But his armor-bearer was terrified and refused to do it. So Saul took his own sword and fell on it.

**v5** When his armor-bearer saw that Saul was dead, he too fell on his own sword and died.
**v6** So Saul died together with his three sons and all his house.
#### The Philistines Possess the Towns

**v7** When all the Israelites in the valley saw that the army had fled and that Saul and his sons had died, they abandoned their cities and ran away. So the Philistines came and occupied their cities.

**v8** The next day, when the Philistines came to strip the dead, they found Saul and his sons fallen on Mount Gilboa.
